title: Bridge Paper: Episodic Visual Hallucinations, Inference and Free Energy.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent Arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Predictive processing — free energy principle, Bayesian brain, active inference, neural causality.
**Phase context:** predictive coding, Bayesian brain, free energy, active inference, predictive processing

Paper: Episodic Visual Hallucinations, Inference and Free Energy.
DOI: 10.3390/e26070557
Abstract: Understandings of how visual hallucinations appear have been highly influenced by generative approaches, in particular Friston's Active Inference conceptualization. Their core proposition is that these phenomena occur when hallucinatory expectations outweigh actual sensory data. This imbalance occurs as the brain seeks to minimize informational free energy, a measure of the distance between predicted and actual sensory data in a stationary open system.
